pages.raw_text:
Travellers will welcome the infor-
nation which the Great Northern
officials give out today. All the num-
erous bridges in the Cascade and
Rocky mountains on that road have
been torn down or repaired. and all
wooden structures replaced with steel
bridges which cannot be carried away
by land and snow slides or undermin-
ed by freshets and thaws.


Perhaps the most disastrous sec
ton of the entire Great Northern sys-
tenn last year was that district near
Boomers Ferry where the kootenai
river dealt out disaster following dis
aster, last spring, accompanied by
loss and wreckage of equipment and
loss of life. So great was the re-
peated catastrophies that at one time
the system had one engine in the
river and three others lying turned
over besides the tracks, and while
the wrecker was attempting to lift
one of them the trackage gave way
and the wrecker went over the bank.
The track was under water for over
a mile.


All these dangers are said to have
since been done away with and the
construction crews have rebuilt the
track for miles.
